name: Ozena, the Suffering and Desperate - Part One: The Yellow Village

text.content:
Ozena, the Suffering and Desperate

Part One: The Yellow Village

A Three-Part Campaign inspired by The Wizard of Oz

Location: Black Forest, West Germany - The Village of Gelb

Duration: 3-4 Sessions per Part

Safety Keywords

This Scenario contains the following sensitive topics that may be uncomfortable for some players:

  • Disfiguring Illness: Characters suffer from diseases that cause severe physical deformity
  • Mental Illness: Characters suffer from psychiatric conditions and delusions
  • Deformity: Characters have severe physical deformities and are outcasts
  • Plagiarism: The antagonist has stolen another person's work and identity

Make sure all players are comfortable with these themes before beginning. Establish safety tools (X-Card, Lines & Veils) at Session Zero.

Campaign Overview

Ozena the Suffering and Desperate is a three-part Campaign that takes the Hunters through the Black Forest in pursuit of a desperate woman seeking a cure for her disfiguring disease. Inspired by The Wizard of Oz, each part features a different witch (Yellow, Red, and Emerald) and explores themes of suffering, desperation, and the lengths people will go to for healing.

The campaign can be played episodically (each part standalone) or as a continuous narrative. Between episodes, the Order can provide support items to help the Hunters.

The Story So Far

Dorothea Gray grew up in early 1900s America, a young woman with dreams of becoming a doctor. But her life was destroyed when she contracted ozena—a terrible disease that slowly destroyed her nasal cavity, leaving her face disfigured and her life in ruins.

Desperate for a cure, Dorothea sought out Dr. Norman Henkle, a brilliant but unscrupulous researcher who claimed to have discovered a treatment. What Henkle actually possessed was something far more dangerous: a partially completed artifact called the Compass Rose, capable of throwing people through time and space.

The incomplete Compass Rose malfunctioned, throwing Dorothea and three other patients—along with Dr. Henkle—backward in time to 1780, into the heart of the Black Forest in Germany. The four patients found themselves trapped in a fairy tale world, their modern diseases incomprehensible to the people of the 18th century.

Dr. Henkle quickly adapted, using his knowledge of future medicine (particularly penicillin) to establish himself as a holy man wizard in the village of Smaragd at the center of the Black Forest. He promised Dorothea he would find a way to cure her and return them home if she helped him gather the missing pieces of the Compass Rose.

The Compass Rose had been shattered and its four pieces given to four witches who formed a coven: Johanne in Schwart (Black Village, North), Margareta in Gelb (Yellow Village, West), Helga in Rot (Red Village, South), and Griselda in Smaragd (Emerald Village, Center). Henkle convinced Dorothea that the witches were evil and must be eliminated to retrieve the Rose pieces.

Dorothea, desperate and suffering, became OZena—a killer who hunts witches while accompanied by three other displaced patients: Lowe (suffering from leontiasis, making him look like a cowardly lion), Hirnloser (a psychiatric patient convinced he has no brain), and Dose (afflicted with leprosy, encased in metal armor like a tin man).

When the Campaign Begins

OZena has already killed Johanne in the Black Village of Schiltach. Now she sets her sights on Margareta Westen, the Good Witch of the Yellow Village of Gelb, who runs a haven for outcasts and the deformed—people just like Dorothea and her companions.

FIRST SCENE - Approaching Gelb

The Hunters are traveling through the Black Forest toward Gelb, following rumors of Margareta Westen, a witch who provides shelter to outcasts. The Order wants to investigate her—is she a threat, or could she be an ally?

As the Hunters approach Gelb, they encounter two farmers on the road: Henrich (an older man) and his adult son Klaus. Both appear nervous and are hurrying away from the village.

The Encounter

Henrich explains that his son Klaus suffers from ozena—a terrible disease that has destroyed his nose. They heard Margareta might help, but when they arrived in Gelb, they were terrified by the sight of so many deformed and disfigured people living there.

Klaus is ashamed and keeps his face covered. If the Hunters are kind and offer to help, Henrich agrees to guide them to Gelb. If they threaten or intimidate him, he runs away.

Note: Unbeknownst to the Hunters, they are being followed by Lowe the Coward, one of OZena's companions. Lowe is observing them from a distance, trying to determine if they are allies of Margareta or potential threats to OZena's plans.

NPCs

Henrich, the worried father
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a simple farmer who loves his son more than anything. I will do whatever it takes to help him, even if it means venturing into a village full of monsters.

Klaus, the afflicted son
OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I suffer from ozena, a disease that has destroyed my nose and made me hideous. I keep my face covered because I cannot bear to see the disgust in people's eyes.

pages.name: The Village of Gelb - Scene Two

text.content:
SECOND SCENE - The Village of Gelb

Gelb is a small village deep in the Black Forest, home to perhaps fifty people. What immediately strikes the Hunters is that nearly everyone here suffers from some form of deformity, disease, or disfigurement. These are the outcasts of society—people with leontiasis, elephantiasis, severe burns, missing limbs, and other conditions that have made them unwelcome elsewhere.

Yet despite their appearances, the villagers seem content. They go about their daily work without shame, and there's a sense of community and acceptance here that's rare in the outside world.

Margareta Appears

As the Hunters explore the village, an elegant woman in yellow robes approaches. This is Margareta Westen, the Good Witch of Gelb. She welcomes the Hunters warmly and invites them to stay as her guests.

Margareta is genuinely kind and explains her mission: to provide a safe haven for those rejected by society. She uses her magic to ease their pain and her wealth to provide for their needs. She asks what brings the Hunters to her village.

Margareta's Demeanor: Warm, maternal, genuinely caring. She seems to be exactly what she claims—a good person using magic to help others.

The Dance

That evening, Margareta hosts a gathering in the village square. A strange figure appears—Hirnloser, a man in ragged clothes who dances wildly. The villagers seem entertained by him, but there's something unsettling about his movements.

Hirnloser approaches the Hunters and speaks in riddles:

Before the Hunters can question him further, he dances away into the night.

The Kidnapping

Shortly after midnight, screams echo through Gelb. Klaus has been kidnapped! Henrich is beside himself with grief. Witnesses saw two figures—one who moved like a dancing scarecrow, another whose body was metal and clanked with every step—carrying Klaus into the swamp west of the village.

Margareta is horrified and begs the Hunters to rescue Klaus. She offers to reward them with information about the Compass Rose artifact and the coven of four witches, if they're interested in such things.

Margareta Westen, The Good Witch

OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a wealthy noblewoman who was born with a facial deformity. Society rejected me, so I use my magic and my fortune to create a haven where the rejected can live in peace and dignity.

Agenda: Protect my village and the outcasts who live here / Use my magic to ease suffering / Stop OZena before she kills more witches.

Gift - Witch

Margareta is a true witch with mastery over two branches of magic:

Emotion Manipulation: Margareta can perform a Check to alter the emotional state of anyone she can see. She can make them feel calm, afraid, angry, or loving. This lasts for one Scene. Victims can resist with a Check against her Opposition Level.

Telekinesis: Margareta can move objects with her mind. She can lift and throw items weighing up to 200 pounds. In combat, she can use this to disarm opponents, create barriers, or hurl objects as weapons (treating this as a normal attack).

Roleplaying Margareta

  • Voice: Warm and maternal, with a slight German accent
  • Mannerisms: Touches people gently when speaking to them, maintains eye contact, smiles despite her facial deformity
  • Motivation: Genuinely wants to help outcasts, has built a community where the rejected can thrive
  • Secret: She knows OZena was once a good person driven mad by desperation
  • Weakness: Her compassion makes her vulnerable—she hesitates to harm even those who threaten her

What Margareta Knows

  • The Compass Rose is a powerful artifact split into four pieces
  • She, Johanne, Helga, and Griselda each hold one piece as protection
  • Johanne was murdered two weeks ago in Schiltach
  • The killer is a woman named Dorothea who calls herself OZena
  • OZena travels with three companions: Lowe, Hirnloser, and Dose
  • Dr. Henkle is manipulating OZena from his fortress in Smaragd

THIRD SCENE - In the Swamp

The swamp west of Gelb is a maze of murky water, twisted trees, and treacherous ground. Following Klaus's trail, the Hunters navigate through the mist until they reach a small clearing on solid ground.

Here they find Klaus, bound and gagged, guarded by two bizarre figures:

  • Hirnloser: A thin man in ragged clothes who moves in jerky, unsettling ways. He speaks constantly in riddles and rhymes.
  • Dose: A massive figure entirely encased in metal armor that's been crudely bolted and strapped onto his body. His movements are stiff and mechanical.

The Trap

This is a trap. Hirnloser and Dose are testing the Hunters:

  • If the Hunters attack immediately, Hirnloser and Dose fight back
  • If the Hunters try to negotiate or ask questions, Hirnloser speaks in riddles about OZena's suffering
  • If the Hunters show compassion or understanding, both captors hesitate

The key insight: Hirnloser and Dose are victims too. They're not evil—they're desperate people trapped in terrible circumstances, following OZena because they believe she's their only hope of getting home.

Lowe Appears

During the confrontation, Lowe the Coward appears. He's been observing the Hunters and tries to mediate. He explains:

  • They're all trapped in this time period
  • Dr. Henkle promised to send them home if they help gather the Compass Rose pieces
  • Dorothea (OZena) is suffering terribly and growing more desperate
  • They don't want to hurt anyone, but they're running out of options

Lowe offers a deal: if the Hunters convince Margareta to give up her piece of the Compass Rose peacefully, they'll release Klaus unharmed. If the Hunters refuse, they'll take Klaus to OZena and let her decide his fate.

NPCs

Lowe the Coward
OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: I suffer from leontiasis, a disease that makes my face look like a lion's. People call me a coward because I adapt to survive—I'll side with whoever is winning.

Agenda: Survive and get home / Protect my companions / Avoid unnecessary violence.

Note: Lowe is actually the most rational of OZena's companions. He questions her increasingly violent methods but fears abandoning her.

Hirnloser, The Scarecrow

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a psychiatric patient from the early 1900s. The doctors said I was mad, that my brain didn't work right. Now I believe I have no brain at all, but I see things others cannot—the truth beneath the surface.

Agenda: Follow OZena because she gives me purpose / Understand the truth of this strange world / Prove I'm more than the madman they say I am.

Roleplaying Hirnloser

  • Speech: Speaks in riddles, rhymes, and fragmented sentences
  • Mannerisms: Jerky movements, tilts head at odd angles, stares intensely
  • Insight: Despite his madness, he often perceives truths others miss
  • Development: In Part Three, he becomes The Genius when he realizes his mind is his greatest gift

Dose, The Tin Man

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I suffer from leprosy and other diseases. To protect others from contagion, I sealed myself in metal armor. I cannot feel anything through the metal, and I fear I am becoming hollow inside, losing my humanity piece by piece.

Agenda: Protect my companions / Find a cure so I can feel again / Prevent myself from becoming a monster.

Gift - Of Steel

Dose's entire body (except for a small door over his heart) is covered in crude metal armor bolted and strapped onto him. He is immune to all physical attacks unless:

  • An attacker specifically targets the heart door (requires 1 Increment from their Outcome)
  • An attack is magical or supernatural in nature

When the heart door is opened or targeted, Dose loses this immunity for the rest of the Scene.

Gift - Giant

Dose has 1 additional Wound beyond what his importance would normally grant.

Roleplaying Dose

  • Voice: Muffled and echoing through his helmet
  • Mannerisms: Stiff, mechanical movements; constantly adjusts his armor
  • Pathos: Desperately wants to feel something, anything
  • Development: In Part Three, he falls in love and his heart returns

FOURTH SCENE - OZena's Attack

The confrontation in the swamp is interrupted by OZena herself. She appears suddenly, stepping out of the mist wearing silver shoes that seem to sparkle with unnatural light.

OZena is a tragic figure. Her face is horribly disfigured by ozena—her nose is gone, leaving a gaping cavity. She covers the lower half of her face with a silver mask. Her eyes are haunted and desperate.

The Assassination Attempt

If the Hunters brought Margareta with them (or if she followed them), OZena attempts to assassinate the witch. She uses her Silver Slippers to teleport directly to Margareta's side and strikes with a poisoned blade.

If Margareta is not present, OZena demands that the Hunters bring her the witch's piece of the Compass Rose. She gives them until dawn.

The Confrontation

This Scene should be dramatic and morally complex:

  • OZena is clearly suffering and desperate
  • She genuinely believes killing the witches is necessary to get home
  • Her companions are torn between loyalty and growing doubts
  • Margareta, if present, shows compassion even to her would-be killer

The Hunters must choose:

  • Fight OZena and her companions
  • Try to negotiate or find a compromise
  • Protect Margareta at all costs
  • Investigate Dr. Henkle's true motives

Possible Outcomes

If OZena is defeated: She escapes using her Silver Slippers, teleporting away. Her companions may surrender or flee depending on how the fight went.

If OZena succeeds: Margareta is killed, and OZena takes her piece of the Compass Rose. She teleports away, leaving her companions to cover her escape.

If a compromise is reached: Margareta may agree to give up her piece of the Compass Rose if OZena agrees to meet with the Hunters and hear the truth about Dr. Henkle.

OZena (Dorothea Gray)

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I suffer from ozena, a disease that destroyed my nose and my life. I was thrown back in time, and the only way home is to gather the pieces of the Compass Rose. I will do whatever it takes to end my suffering—even murder.

Agenda: Collect all four pieces of the Compass Rose / Get home to my own time / Find a cure for my disease.

Gift - The Silver Slippers

OZena wears a pair of enchanted silver shoes that allow her to teleport.

Short-Range Teleport: Once per Exchange, OZena can teleport up to 30 feet to any location she can see. This doesn't count as an action and can be used to avoid attacks or reposition instantly.

Long-Distance Teleport: Once per Scene, OZena can teleport to any location within 1 mile that she has previously visited. This requires her full action.

The Silver Slippers are permanently attached to her feet and cannot be removed without killing her.

Roleplaying OZena

  • Voice: Muffled by her mask, tinged with pain and desperation
  • Mannerisms: Keeps one hand on her mask, moves with purpose, eyes constantly scanning
  • Motivation: She was a good person broken by suffering and manipulation
  • Tragedy: Every witch she kills makes her more monstrous, but she's too desperate to stop
  • Redemption: She can be saved if the Hunters show her the truth about Henkle

What OZena Knows

  • Dr. Henkle has the ability to send people through time using the Compass Rose
  • The Compass Rose needs all four pieces to function safely
  • Johanne, Margareta, Helga, and Griselda each hold one piece
  • Henkle told her the witches are evil and must be eliminated
  • She's already killed Johanne and taken her piece

What OZena Doesn't Know

  • Henkle is lying about the witches being evil
  • The incomplete Compass Rose is more dangerous than helpful
  • Henkle plans to abandon her once he has what he needs
  • Her actions are part of a larger scheme orchestrated by the One Who Plots in the Shadows

Epilogue: The Compass Rose

The Artifact

The Compass Rose is an ancient artifact of immense power, capable of creating portals through time and space. It was created by a powerful wizard centuries ago and has been passed down through generations of magic users.

The artifact resembles a compass rose made of four interlocking pieces, each corresponding to a cardinal direction:

  • North (Black): Held by Johanne of Schiltach (deceased)
  • West (Yellow): Held by Margareta of Gelb
  • South (Red): Held by Helga of Rot
  • Center (Emerald): Held by Griselda of Smaragd (actually stolen by Henkle)

The Coven's Purpose

The four witches formed a coven not for evil purposes, but to protect the Compass Rose. They split it into four pieces because they knew that when united, it could be used to:

  • Summon terrible storms
  • Throw people between worlds and times
  • Tear holes in reality itself

Each witch took a piece and founded a village where she could guard it while helping those in need. They believed that as long as the pieces remained separated, the artifact was safe.

What Happened

Dr. Norman Henkle, a brilliant but amoral researcher from early 1900s America, discovered the Compass Rose through stolen research. He manipulated several patients (including Dorothea) into helping him activate it.

But Henkle only had access to two pieces, making the Compass Rose unstable. When he activated it, instead of controlled time travel, it threw everyone backward 120 years into the Black Forest of 1780.

Now Henkle is trying to gather all four pieces, convinced that a complete Compass Rose will allow him to return home—or perhaps conquer this time period entirely.

The Truth About the Witches

The four witches are not evil:

  • Johanne: Provided medicine and healing to the people of Schiltach
  • Margareta: Created a haven for outcasts and the deformed
  • Helga: Her story will be revealed in Part Two
  • Griselda: Her story will be revealed in Part Three

They are guardians trying to prevent the Compass Rose from falling into the wrong hands. OZena has been manipulated into becoming their killer.

Concluding Part One

Possible Endings

Margareta Survives: She thanks the Hunters and warns them about Helga in Rot to the south. She gives them information about the Compass Rose and urges them to stop Henkle before he gathers all four pieces. She may give them her piece for safekeeping.

Margareta Dies: The village of Gelb mourns their protector. The outcasts are terrified of what will happen to them now. OZena escapes with Margareta's piece and heads south toward Rot. The Hunters must decide whether to pursue her or report back to the Order.

OZena is Captured: If the Hunters manage to capture OZena, she can be reasoned with. She's desperate and frightened, but not irredeemable. If shown evidence of Henkle's manipulation, she may become an ally. However, her companions will fight to free her if necessary.

A Compromise: Perhaps Margareta and OZena reach an understanding. Margareta might agree to help OZena return home in exchange for stopping the murders. This creates an uneasy alliance heading into Part Two.

Foreshadowing Part Two

No matter how Part One ends, the Hunters learn that Helga, the Red Witch of Rot, is OZena's next target. Helga is very different from Margareta—she's more powerful, more secretive, and her village is a much darker place.

Rumors suggest:

  • Helga practices blood magic
  • Strange creatures serve her
  • The people of Rot worship her like a goddess
  • No one who crosses her lives long

The Hunters must decide: Will they try to save Helga, or are they too late?

Scenario Events

  • If the Hunters are kind to Henrich and Klaus: Lowe observes this and gains respect for them. He's more likely to negotiate in the swamp scene.
  • If the Hunters attack OZena's companions without trying to negotiate: Lowe becomes convinced they're no better than the witch hunters of this era and fights to the death.
  • If the Hunters try to understand OZena's suffering: She hesitates and may be willing to talk. This is the first step toward her eventual redemption.
  • If Margareta is killed: The entire village of Gelb falls into despair. Some outcasts may turn hostile, blaming the Hunters for not protecting her.
  • If the Hunters investigate Henkle: They can learn he's established himself in Smaragd as a holy man and wizard. Locals speak of his miraculous cures but also mention strange experiments.
  • If the Hunters capture Hirnloser: He speaks in riddles but reveals important truths:

• If the Hunters show Dose compassion: He's deeply moved and may refuse to fight them later. His humanity is the key to his redemption.
  • If the Hunters retrieve Margareta's piece of the Compass Rose: It resembles a yellow compass needle made of amber. It hums with magical energy and points persistently toward Smaragd.

flags.setting: Black Forest, Germany - 1780 - The Village of Gelb
